title: hamamelidaceae
intro: The taxonomical rank of Hamamelidaceae refers to a family of flowering plants. It is a group of plants characterized by their woody habit and often aromatic, star-shaped flowers. The family Hamamelidaceae includes about 90 species, commonly found in temperate regions with high humidity, such as tropical and subtropical Asia and North America. Some notable genera within this family include Hamamelis (witch hazel) and Liquidambar (sweetgum).
